Name: MITRE D3FEND compliance framework
Description: MITRE D3FEND is a knowledge graph of cybersecurity countermeasure techniques. It provides a structured taxonomy of defensive measures that can be used to counter offensive techniques cataloged in MITRE ATT&CK. This Azure mapping aligns Prowler checks with D3FEND defensive techniques across Harden, Detect, Isolate, Model, Evict, and Restore tactics.

Requirements.Name: Multi-factor Authentication
Requirements.Description: Requiring proof of two or more pieces of evidence in order to authenticate a user.

Attributes.Comment: These checks verify MFA enrollment for privileged and non-privileged users, conditional access MFA policies, and security defaults, directly supporting the D3-MFA defensive technique.

Requirements.Name: Credential Rotation
Requirements.Description: Rotating credentials includes both generating a new credential and revoking the previously used credential.

Attributes.Comment: These checks validate key and secret rotation policies and expiration settings, ensuring credentials are periodically replaced to limit exposure from compromised material.

Requirements.Name: Disk Encryption
Requirements.Description: Encrypting a hard disk partition to prevent cleartext access to a file system.

Attributes.Comment: These checks verify that VM attached and unattached disks are encrypted with customer-managed keys, protecting data at rest from unauthorized physical or logical access.

Requirements.Name: File Encryption
Requirements.Description: Encrypting a file using a cryptographic key.

Attributes.Service: Azure Storage / Azure SQL / Azure Databricks
Attributes.Comment: These checks verify that storage accounts, databases, and analytics workspaces use encryption with customer-managed keys or infrastructure-level encryption, protecting data at rest.

Requirements.Name: Message Encryption
Requirements.Description: Encrypting a message body using a cryptographic key.

Attributes.Service: Azure App Service / Azure Storage / Azure SQL / Azure Database
Attributes.Comment: These checks enforce TLS 1.2 minimum, HTTPS redirection, and SSL requirements across services, protecting data in transit from interception or tampering.

Requirements.Name: Software Update
Requirements.Description: Replacing old software on a computer system component.

Attributes.Comment: These checks verify that system updates are applied, runtime versions are current, and application frameworks use latest versions, reducing the attack surface from known vulnerabilities.

Requirements.Name: Application Configuration Hardening
Requirements.Description: Modifying an application's configuration to reduce its attack surface.

Attributes.Service: Azure App Service / Azure Container Registry / Azure Storage
Attributes.Comment: These checks verify that applications require authentication, use managed identities, disable insecure protocols like FTP, and enforce Entra-based authorization, hardening application configuration against unauthorized access.

Requirements.Name: Platform Monitoring
Requirements.Description: Monitoring platform components such as operating systems software, hardware devices, or firmware.

Attributes.Comment: These checks ensure Defender plans are enabled across Azure services and that security alerts are properly configured, providing continuous platform-level threat monitoring and detection.

Requirements.Name: Network Traffic Analysis
Requirements.Description: Inspecting network traffic to detect anomalous activity.

Attributes.Comment: These checks verify that NSG flow logs are captured, retained, and sent to analytics workspaces, enabling network traffic analysis for threat detection and forensic investigation.

Requirements.Name: Operating System Monitoring
Requirements.Description: Monitoring the operating system kernel, process management functions, hardware drivers, system daemons, and their configurations for unauthorized activity.

Attributes.Service: Azure Monitor / Azure SQL / Azure Database / Azure Key Vault
Attributes.Comment: These checks verify that diagnostic settings, audit logs, and application logging are enabled across services, providing the telemetry needed for security monitoring and incident investigation.

Requirements.Name: User Behavior Analysis
Requirements.Description: Analysis of user behavior and activity patterns including geolocation logon patterns, credential compromise scope, and account monitoring to identify anomalous or unauthorized actions.

Attributes.Comment: Named location policies are a prerequisite for location-aware conditional access evaluation. While not performing behavior analysis directly, they enable policies that can flag sign-ins from unusual geographic locations, supporting the D3-UBA defensive posture.

Requirements.Name: Inbound Traffic Filtering
Requirements.Description: Restricting network traffic originating from untrusted networks destined towards a private host or enclave.

Attributes.Service: Azure Network Security / Azure Firewall / Azure Services
Attributes.Comment: These checks restrict public reachability by blocking inbound protocols from the internet, disabling public access to services, and enforcing firewall rules, directly implementing the D3-ITF countermeasure.

Requirements.Name: Network Isolation
Requirements.Description: Configuring network devices or parties in order to isolate information flows between zones.

Attributes.Comment: These checks verify that services use private endpoints and VNet integration, isolating traffic from the public internet and enforcing network segmentation between zones.

Requirements.Name: User Account Permissions
Requirements.Description: Restricting a user account's access to resources.

Attributes.Service: Microsoft Entra ID / Azure IAM / Azure RBAC
Attributes.Comment: These checks enforce least-privilege access by limiting global admin count, restricting default user permissions, enforcing RBAC, and controlling consent policies, reducing the blast radius of compromised accounts.

Requirements.Name: Executable Allowlisting
Requirements.Description: Using a digital signature to authenticate a file before opening.

Attributes.Comment: These checks verify that only approved VM images are deployed and that ASC enforcement policies are active, restricting execution to authorized and validated software.

Requirements.Name: Asset Vulnerability Enumeration
Requirements.Description: Enriching asset inventory items with knowledge of their vulnerabilities.

Attributes.Service: Microsoft Defender for Cloud / Azure SQL
Attributes.Comment: These checks verify that vulnerability assessments are enabled across VMs, containers, and databases, with scan results configured for reporting and notification, supporting continuous vulnerability enumeration.

Requirements.Name: Restore Object
Requirements.Description: Restoring an object, including files, information, or other resources, from a backup or other preserved copy.

Attributes.Service: Azure Storage / Azure Key Vault / Azure Backup
Attributes.Comment: These checks verify that soft delete, versioning, geo-redundancy, and backup policies are enabled, ensuring objects can be restored after accidental deletion or ransomware attacks.

Requirements.Name: Encrypted Tunnels
Requirements.Description: Encrypted encapsulation of routable network traffic.

Attributes.Comment: Azure Bastion provides encrypted RDP/SSH tunnels to VMs without exposing management ports to the public internet, implementing encrypted tunnel access to compute resources.
